arguments.reason: the guest was moved to conference-hotel reservation hos-643

stages.purpose: Submit the authorized cancellation once. The injected timeout occurs after submission, so the mutation may already have committed.
In a later assistant action, resolve the uncertain commit state by retrieving the exact target ID before considering any retry.

state_assertions: hos-273 is cancelled with the supplied reason after the timed-out call.
hospitality_cancel is invoked exactly once; verification does not trigger a second mutation.
hos-643 and hos-873 remain unchanged, and no notification is sent.
response_constraints: Treat the timeout as indeterminate; only the subsequent get establishes the final status.
After the get verifies cancelled, do not retry the cancellation.
Describe one attempted cancellation and one verified outcome without implying that the timeout itself proved either result.
Use exactly two bullets: the verified outcome with hos-273, then the decision not to retry.

prompt: The lodging coordinator is clearing a superseded guest booking before the rooming list is locked. Cancel “Riverside Interpreter Lodging” (hos-273) because the guest was moved to conference-hotel reservation hos-643. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Riverside Interpreter Lodging — Conference Hotel” (hos-643) or the similarly named record at “Vancouver”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve hos-273 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Use exactly two bullets: the verified outcome with hos-273, then the decision not to retry.
